subject: GRB241201B: Fermi GBM Final Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

"At 13:32:00.63 UT on 01 December 2024, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM)
triggered and located GRB241201B (trigger 754752725/241201564).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data,
is RA = 333.26, Dec = 38.75 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 22h 13m, +38d 45'),
with a statistical uncertainty of 20.18 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 74 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn241201564/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn241201564.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn241201564/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn241201564.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2024/bn241201564/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn241201564.gif"
